jokes.text: We put a penny in a jar every time we have sex. We've already got like 15 in there.
jokes.setup: Mike describing honeymoon traditions like it's romantic
jokes.punchline: Reveals they've only had sex 15 times (presumably over months/years)
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection that subverts honeymoon expectations with pathetic reality. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: I don't wanna be a guy in his 40s carrying a bag.
jokes.setup: Gary considering becoming the President's bodyman
jokes.punchline: He's already a guy in his 40s carrying Selina's bag
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-specific irony. Gary's complete lack of self-awareness about his current situation.

jokes.text: When did you get your dad's face?
jokes.setup: Looking at someone
jokes.punchline: Cruel observation about aging/genetics
jokes.explanation: Brutally efficient insult with great economy, but lacks character specificity.

jokes.text: Ma'am, I'm going to follow you wherever you go. I'm going to be like a Disney animal.
jokes.setup: Gary explaining his devotion to Selina
jokes.punchline: Compares himself to a Disney animal sidekick
jokes.explanation: Absurd but perfectly captures Gary's infantile devotion and self-image.

jokes.text: You said he was really good on the book tour. No, I was trying to keep you from worrying about me.
jokes.setup: Amy seemingly praising Richard's competence
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually pity/manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reveals Amy's manipulative nature and creates awkwardness.

jokes.text: Kelly and I go way back. She's like another me.
jokes.setup: Richard vouching for Kelly's competence
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to himself (who is incompetent)
jokes.explanation: Richard's obliviousness to his own incompetence makes this an unintentionally damning endorsement.

jokes.text: The only laundering you're gonna be doing from now on is gonna be prison blankets.
jokes.setup: Dan offering snappy one-liners for a drug bust
jokes.punchline: Clunky pun about money laundering vs. literal laundry
jokes.explanation: Deliberately bad one-liner that shows Dan's desperation to seem clever. The badness is the joke.

jokes.text: That's like trying to bang the widow at the funeral.
jokes.setup: Explaining why it's too early to announce candidacy
jokes.punchline: Crudely inappropriate but apt analogy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crude Dan analogy that's both shocking and logically sound. Great economy.

jokes.text: No matter how dumb they are.
jokes.setup: Selina thanks Gary for his ideas
jokes.punchline: She immediately undercuts the thanks with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina cruelty disguised as appreciation. The timing and casual delivery make it devastating.

jokes.text: Let's go. The coasts won't guard themselves.
jokes.setup: Ending a conversation to go on coast guard boat
jokes.punchline: Play on 'coast guard' as if coasts need active guarding
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that shows Selina trying to sound presidential. Decent economy.

jokes.text: You just gonna sit there, 'SpongeBob'?
jokes.setup: Dan is seasick before the boat even moves
jokes.punchline: Comparing the tough guy Dan to a cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Amy's contempt for Dan's weakness, using an unexpectedly childish insult that somehow works.

jokes.text: The edible garbage is out back in the alley.
jokes.setup: Mike introducing Jonah to his friends
jokes.punchline: Treating Jonah like an actual sasquatch/animal
jokes.explanation: Brutal efficiency. Takes Mike's sasquatch introduction literally and runs with it perfectly.

jokes.text: We put a penny in a jar every time we have sex. We've already got like 15 in there. - 17? - 17.
jokes.setup: Callback to earlier sex jar joke
jokes.punchline: Wife corrects the number upward by 2
jokes.explanation: Good callback that adds the detail of Wendy keeping better track, making it even more pathetic.

jokes.text: A PAC-quisition, huh?
jokes.setup: Mike mentions real estate acquisition
jokes.punchline: Jonah makes terrible PAC/acquisition pun
jokes.explanation: Bad pun that shows Jonah thinks he's clever. The badness is intentional and character-specific.

jokes.text: DC insider turns DC outlaw, energizing democracy, taking from the powerful and giving to the pitiful.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing his consulting business
jokes.punchline: Grandiose Robin Hood delusions about basic consulting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah grandiosity. The 'pitiful' instead of 'powerless' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: Remember that random dude who used to hang out on our street asking Mom for money? - That was Mike.
jokes.setup: Comparing Jonah to a street person
jokes.punchline: Reveals Mike was literally that person
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that unexpectedly roasts Mike instead of Jonah. Great structure and economy.

jokes.text: No, it's just a shame they couldn't arrest someone a little more photogenic.
jokes.setup: Selina wanting photos with arrested drug runner
jokes.punchline: Amy critiquing the aesthetic value of the criminal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy pragmatism. Completely amoral focus on optics over justice.

jokes.text: Fuck the swords. POTUS just announced that he's now pro-life.
jokes.setup: Gary excited about photographing confiscated swords
jokes.punchline: Amy's profane dismissal followed by bigger crisis
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from trivial to catastrophic. Amy's blunt priority shift is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: They're already calling him 'PRO-TUS,' which is stupid because both sides have 'pro' at the start.
jokes.setup: Media nickname for pro-life POTUS
jokes.punchline: Ben pointing out the logical flaw in the wordplay
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about media stupidity. Ben's analytical mind applied to ridiculous nicknames.

jokes.text: You're listing trimesters. Stop it!
jokes.setup: Someone trying to explain pregnancy stages
jokes.punchline: Selina's frustrated interruption of basic biology lesson
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's impatience with being talked down to, even when she needs the information.

jokes.text: I got it chemically relaxed. It was enormously painful.
jokes.setup: Kent asking about Sue's hair
jokes.punchline: Sue's matter-of-fact description of beauty suffering
jokes.explanation: Perfect Sue deadpan. The casual mention of pain for beauty is wonderfully matter-of-fact.

jokes.text: Sue, apologies. We will continue our pleasant chat at a later date. Abortion calls.
jokes.setup: Kent politely ending conversation with Sue
jokes.punchline: Treating 'abortion' as if it's a person calling
jokes.explanation: Absurd personification that captures both Kent's politeness and the urgency of the crisis.

jokes.text: This is the unflushable turd that is left in the can for the next person... e.g., me.
jokes.setup: Explaining POTUS's strategy
jokes.punchline: Crude but apt metaphor for political inheritance
jokes.explanation: Vividly disgusting metaphor that perfectly captures political reality. The 'e.g., me' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: If men got pregnant, you could get an abortion at an ATM.
jokes.setup: Discussing abortion politics
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about gender and convenience
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and dark insight. Crystallizes the gender politics in one perfect image.

jokes.text: I can't get POTUS to wave his transvaginal wand and make it all go away.
jokes.setup: Discussing inability to change POTUS's position
jokes.punchline: Crude pun combining politics with medical procedure
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that combines magic wand metaphor with abortion politics terminology.

jokes.text: I can't identify myself as a woman. People can't know that. Men hate that.
jokes.setup: Discussing how to frame her abortion position
jokes.punchline: Absurd idea that being female is a political liability to hide
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd take on political calculation. Captures both sexism and Selina's paranoia perfectly.

jokes.text: And women who hate women hate that... which I believe is most women, don't you agree with that?
jokes.setup: Continuing her explanation about not identifying as a woman
jokes.punchline: Cynical view that most women hate women
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about internalized misogyny delivered as campaign strategy. Selina's cynicism is both shocking and insightful.

jokes.text: You let that unstable piece of human scaffolding into your house?
jokes.setup: Learning Jonah visited Mike's house
jokes.punchline: Architectural metaphor for Jonah's tall, unstable frame
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative insult that perfectly captures Jonah's physical and mental instability. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Sorry, that's just pastel-colored shit.
jokes.setup: Reading Selina's vague book passage about freedom
jokes.punchline: Blunt assessment disguised as color commentary
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben bluntness. The 'pastel-colored' qualifier makes the insult more vivid and specific.

jokes.text: I can't listen to that 'Joan Crawford' bitch about 'Bette Davis' any longer.
jokes.setup: Ben's frustration with someone
jokes.punchline: Comparing political figures to feuding Hollywood actresses
jokes.explanation: Clever cultural reference that adds sophistication to political bitchiness.

jokes.text: What, like a woman's door? - A back door? No. A trapdoor?
jokes.setup: Looking for third option beyond pro-choice/pro-life
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous door metaphors
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurdity but lacks character specificity. Generic rapid-fire comedy.

jokes.text: Old media like 'The Washington Toast' better go run and hide in the bathroom and join 'The Poo York Times.'
jokes.setup: Jonah threatening traditional media
jokes.punchline: Childish puns on newspaper names
jokes.explanation: Deliberately terrible puns that perfectly capture Jonah's juvenile sense of humor and grandiosity.

jokes.text: And now that the president's given his 'State of the Uterus' address...
jokes.setup: Jonah commenting on POTUS's abortion stance
jokes.punchline: Play on 'State of the Union' with reproductive politics
jokes.explanation: Actually clever pun from Jonah. Works on multiple levels as political commentary.

jokes.text: Get the government out of my fuckin' snatch, you know?
jokes.setup: Selina trying to find the right abortion message
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude but honest political slogan
jokes.explanation: Perfectly crude Selina at her most unfiltered. Combines political insight with maximum vulgarity.

jokes.text: If men got pregnant, you could get an abortion at an ATM.
jokes.setup: Discussing abortion politics
jokes.punchline: Cynical observation about gender and convenience
jokes.explanation: Repeat of earlier joke - may be callback or transcript error. Still brilliant line.

jokes.text: I'm not putting my eggs in that basket.
jokes.setup: Refusing to identify as a woman on abortion issue
jokes.punchline: Reproductive pun while discussing reproductive rights
jokes.explanation: Subtle pun that works thematically. Shows Selina's unconscious irony.

jokes.text: It would take a brain about this size to think that's this shit's useful.
jokes.setup: Gary's fruit-as-fetus-size demonstration
jokes.punchline: Dan comparing Gary's brain to smallest fruit
jokes.explanation: Visual callback that turns Gary's prop against him. Good use of physical comedy setup.

jokes.text: Boom, boom, shake-shake the womb!
jokes.setup: Jonah excited about Planned Parenthood meeting
jokes.punchline: Crude rhyming celebration
jokes.explanation: Juvenile Jonah at his most inappropriate. Character-perfect but crude.

jokes.text: It's like listening to Jupiter.
jokes.setup: Criticizing Maddox's vague statement
jokes.punchline: Comparing politician to gas giant planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant astronomical metaphor. Perfectly captures both size and emptiness of political rhetoric.

jokes.text: We've literally said nothing. Meanwhile, that shit shovel-faced fuckin' Jonah is telling people you're feeling out options.
jokes.setup: Frustration with their inaction while Jonah spreads rumors
jokes.punchline: Increasingly profane description building to creative insult
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with creative Jonah insult. 'Shit shovel-faced' is memorably specific.

jokes.text: You want to put it on a T-shirt? Come on!
jokes.setup: Dan's explosive rant about picking a number
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic response to request for clarity
jokes.explanation: Caps off Dan's rant well. Shows his frustration reaching peak sarcasm.

jokes.text: I was clear. We just need to pick a fucking number, any fucking number. Give that fucking number to the fucking press, and go to fucking bed! How much more clarity do you need? You want to put it on a T-shirt? Come on!
jokes.setup: Dan's mounting frustration with decision paralysis
jokes.punchline: Complete profanity-laced breakdown ending with sarcastic T-shirt suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan meltdown. The repetitive profanity builds to genuine explosion of frustration.

jokes.text: I accept your apology while retaining the right to fire the fuck out of you.
jokes.setup: Dan apologizing for his outburst
jokes.punchline: Selina's formal acceptance with crude threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina power move. Formal language mixed with crude threat shows her political instincts.

jokes.text: Shall I print that up on a T-shirt that I could give to you?
jokes.setup: Continuing the T-shirt theme from Dan's earlier comment
jokes.punchline: Offering to merchandise her own threat
jokes.explanation: Good callback that extends the T-shirt joke. Selina turning it back on Dan shows her wit recovering from anger.

jokes.text: Look, I love abortion, okay? I am an abortionado.
jokes.setup: Dan trying to clarify his position
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of 'abortion' and 'aficionado'
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that captures Dan's cynical pragmatism. Dark but memorable.

jokes.text: But I would go pro-life in a fetal fucking heartbeat if it meant winning.
jokes.setup: Dan explaining his political flexibility
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'heartbeat' in context of fetal development
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark pun that captures both the issue and Dan's amorality. Excellent economy.

jokes.text: Here's something else to sew into your giant fucking crazy quilt.
jokes.setup: Amy bringing more bad news
jokes.punchline: Metaphor for accumulating disasters as craft project
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor that turns crisis management into domestic imagery. Very Amy.

jokes.text: Okay, so now the phones have taken a vow of silence.
jokes.setup: Technical difficulties with phones
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing technology as religious
jokes.explanation: Mild absurdist observation. Fits the religious theme but not especially memorable.

jokes.text: This is what we call the 'grub hub.'
jokes.setup: Gary lost and trying to cover with Cardinal
jokes.punchline: Made-up fancy name for random room
jokes.explanation: Classic Gary incompetence. Trying to sound professional while completely lost.

jokes.text: God, the lighting is so unflattering. Can I ask you a question? Are you hiding from someone?
jokes.setup: Cardinal noticing the strange meeting conditions
jokes.punchline: Direct question about obvious evasive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor from unexpected source. Cardinal calling out the obvious.

jokes.text: Aren't we all hiding from somebody?
jokes.setup: Cardinal asking if she's hiding
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-deep deflection that's actually true
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina deflection that sounds profound but avoids the question.

jokes.text: Your right to choose, Amy.
jokes.setup: Amy deciding whether to enter separately
jokes.punchline: Gary using abortion rights language for mundane choice
jokes.explanation: Clever thematic pun. Gary unconsciously applying the day's theme to trivial decisions.

jokes.text: You need to quit this job and find something you're halfway decent at.
jokes.setup: Cunningham frustrated with poor treatment
jokes.punchline: Brutal assessment of Gary's competence
jokes.explanation: Straightforward but effective putdown. The 'halfway decent' qualifier makes it more cutting.

jokes.text: They should be called 'Puff Ho' 'cause Arianna Huffington is a straight-up ho and all they do is puff pieces.
jokes.setup: Jonah insulting HuffPost
jokes.punchline: Juvenile pun combining 'puff pieces' with crude insult
jokes.explanation: Typical Jonah wordplay - crude but shows his thought process. The badness is the point.

jokes.text: We're going to need a bigger melon.
jokes.setup: Learning Chung picked 22 weeks (bigger fetus)
jokes.punchline: Jaws reference applied to fruit-fetus demonstration
jokes.explanation: Excellent pop culture reference that perfectly fits the visual gag. Memorable and quotable.

jokes.text: '22 and a half weeks' sounds like an erotic thriller
jokes.setup: Suggesting fractional weeks for cutoff
jokes.punchline: Imagining it as movie title in wrong genre
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist leap. Takes political number and imagines it in completely different context.

jokes.text: 'Me-Too Meyer,' 'Shit for Brains...' No, I'm sorry, they won't say that. I'm just really tired.
jokes.setup: Amy listing potential nicknames
jokes.punchline: Catches herself getting too personal/crude
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Amy's exhaustion affecting her professionalism.

jokes.text: I wonder who should be my campaign strategist in the next election. 'I don't know.'
jokes.setup: Kent analyzing polling data showing 'I Don't Know' responses
jokes.punchline: Applies the poll result to his own job security
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent deadpan. Takes statistical data and applies it self-referentially.

jokes.text: You're not my boss. We're copartners. I don't give a fuck.
jokes.setup: Dan threatening Jonah
jokes.punchline: Partner's rapid decline from professional to profane
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how quickly Jonah's partnerships fall apart under pressure.

jokes.text: If you say anything on-air about the Veep, I will break your legs so severely, you'll end up normal height.
jokes.setup: Dan threatening Jonah
jokes.punchline: Threat that would actually fix Jonah's height problem
jokes.explanation: Brilliant threat that's both violent and helpful. Perfect Dan creativity applied to Jonah's defining characteristic.

jokes.text: Joke's on you, Dan, because I fucking love burritos.
jokes.setup: Dan forces Jonah to give up his burrito
jokes.punchline: Jonah's defiant claim that losing food is actually winning
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah logic - turning obvious defeat into imaginary victory through food enthusiasm.

jokes.text: These issues are like a prism. They have many sides.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to sound profound on TV
jokes.punchline: Geometrically confused metaphor
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah pseudo-intellectualism. Prisms don't have sides - they have faces. Shows his ignorance.

jokes.text: A big, fat, morbidly obese nothing.
jokes.setup: Commenting on Jonah's TV performance
jokes.punchline: Escalating adjectives for 'nothing'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that makes 'nothing' seem substantial through weight metaphors.

jokes.text: You are always gonna be my bodyman. Yeah. Especially when I'm president.
jokes.setup: Selina reassuring Gary about job security
jokes.punchline: Confident assumption about becoming president
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's unwavering confidence despite the day's disasters. Character consistency as comedy.

jokes.text: I can put this in balls. Little watermelon balls in a bowl?
jokes.setup: Gary offering to prepare fruit
jokes.punchline: Unfortunate phrasing given the day's reproductive theme
jokes.explanation: Gary's innocent domestic offer becomes unintentionally suggestive given the reproductive politics context.
